about.title: About VaneHub AI
about.description: Review software details, project links, changelog, and version status
about.productSummary: VaneHub AI is a desktop terminal for managing multiple AI coding assistants, unifying Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and local development workflows.
about.software.title: Software Details
about.software.description: Current app identity, release channel, and project metadata
about.changelog.title: Recent Changes
about.changelog.description: Product capabilities emphasized in the current preview
about.changelog.item1: The unified settings center now covers CLI, SDK, MCP, Agent, Skill, and basic configuration management.
about.changelog.item2: The desktop runtime hosts local detection, registry, session, and logging capabilities through Tauri while Web keeps a previewable mock adapter.
about.changelog.item3: Multi-agent sessions, workspace worktrees, unified logging, and local dependency management continue to be refined.
about.update.title: Version Updates
about.update.description: Check the latest available version from GitHub Releases
about.update.current: You are on the latest version v{{version}}
about.runtime.title: Runtime and Agents
about.runtime.description: Supported runtime shapes and AI coding assistant ecosystem
about.highlights.title: Product Focus
about.highlights.multiAgent: Manage multiple AI coding assistants in one place and reduce switching costs across CLIs and workflows.
about.highlights.localFirst: A local-first desktop architecture where the frontend adapts to Tauri and Web runtimes through service boundaries.

usage.title: Usage Statistics
usage.description: Monitor reported tokens and estimated character usage across VaneHub-managed sessions
usage.empty: No usage records in the selected time range
usage.range.label: Statistics time range
usage.reported.totalHint: CLI-reported input, output, and cache tokens combined
usage.reported.outputHint: Includes reasoning output reported by the CLI
usage.estimated.totalHint: Input and output characters recorded when tokens are unavailable; never added to tokens
usage.coverage.title: Reported Coverage
usage.coverage.hint: Share of assistant responses with CLI-reported tokens
usage.sessions.title: Sessions
usage.sessions.hint: Sessions containing at least one usage record
usage.trend.title: Daily Trend
usage.trend.description: Tokens and characters use separate scales so different units are never mixed
usage.agents.title: Usage by Agent
usage.agents.description: Grouped by stable Agent ID, independent of display-name changes
usage.accounting.title: Accounting Notes
usage.accounting.description: Understand data sources, units, and current boundaries
usage.accounting.reported: Reported tokens come from structured CLI usage events and deduplicate repeated or cumulative observations.
usage.accounting.estimated: Successful responses without reported usage record input and output character counts only; characters are never converted or added to tokens.
usage.accounting.limitations: Only VaneHub-managed sessions are counted; external CLI history, provider billing reconciliation, cost estimates, request-detail logs, and Provider/model filtering are not included.

cliParameters.title: CLI Parameter Management
cliParameters.description: Configure structured startup parameters for four coding CLIs. Saved defaults are injected into later interactive and chat launches.
cliParameters.error.invalidValue: The value for parameter {{parameter}} is invalid.
cliParameters.agents.title: Managed CLI
cliParameters.agents.description: Parameters are stored separately for each CLI.
cliParameters.empty: No parameters match the current search.
cliParameters.preview.title: Safe argument preview
cliParameters.preview.description: Shows only managed, non-secret arguments. The executable and prompt are intentionally omitted.
cliParameters.values.default.label: Use CLI default
cliParameters.values.default.description: Do not add an override for this parameter.
cliParameters.claude-code.model.label: Model
cliParameters.claude-code.model.descript: Select the Claude model alias passed with --model.
cliParameters.claude-code.model.values.d: Let Claude Code choose its configured default model.
cliParameters.claude-code.effort.label: Reasoning effort
cliParameters.claude-code.effort.descrip: Control how much reasoning effort Claude uses before responding.
cliParameters.claude-code.permissionMode: Choose how Claude requests or applies tool permissions.
Use the CLI's configured permission behavior.
Plan first and wait before making changes.
Let Claude Code choose the permission behavior.
Do not prompt for permissions that are not already granted.
cliParameters.claude-code.chrome.label: Chrome integration
cliParameters.claude-code.chrome.descrip: Enable Claude Code's Chrome integration for interactive launches.
cliParameters.codex-cli.model.label: Model
cliParameters.codex-cli.model.descriptio: Select the OpenAI model passed to Codex CLI.
cliParameters.codex-cli.model.values.def: Use the model configured by Codex CLI.
cliParameters.codex-cli.model.values.gpt: Use GPT-5.5 when it is available in the installed CLI.
Use the GPT-5.4 general reasoning model.
Use the coding-optimized GPT-5.2 Codex model.
Use the long-running coding model where supported.
Set the validated model_reasoning_effort configuration value.
Use Codex CLI's configured reasoning effort.
cliParameters.codex-cli.sandbox.label: Sandbox
cliParameters.codex-cli.sandbox.descript: Limit filesystem access available to generated commands.
cliParameters.codex-cli.sandbox.values.d: Use the sandbox policy from Codex configuration.
cliParameters.codex-cli.sandbox.values.r: Allow reads but prevent workspace writes.
cliParameters.codex-cli.sandbox.values.w: Allow writes inside the selected workspace.
cliParameters.codex-cli.approvalPolicy.d: Choose when Codex asks before running generated commands.
cliParameters.codex-cli.approvalPolicy.v: Use the approval policy from Codex configuration.
Prompt for commands outside the trusted set.
Let Codex request elevated execution when needed.
Never request approval; failures remain inside the configured sandbox.
cliParameters.codex-cli.ephemeral.label: Ephemeral session
cliParameters.codex-cli.ephemeral.descri: Do not persist this Codex chat runtime session.
cliParameters.codex-cli.strictConfig.des: Treat invalid Codex configuration values as launch errors.
cliParameters.gemini-cli.model.label: Model
cliParameters.gemini-cli.model.descripti: Select the Gemini model passed with --model.
cliParameters.gemini-cli.model.values.de: Use the model configured by Gemini CLI.
cliParameters.gemini-cli.approvalMode.de: Choose how Gemini approves edits and tool calls.
cliParameters.gemini-cli.approvalMode.va: Use Gemini CLI's configured approval behavior.
Plan first without automatically applying changes.
Automatically approve all tool calls; use only in trusted workspaces.
cliParameters.gemini-cli.sandbox.label: Sandbox
cliParameters.gemini-cli.sandbox.descrip: Run Gemini tools through its sandbox where supported.
cliParameters.opencode.agent.label: Agent
cliParameters.opencode.agent.description: Choose the OpenCode agent profile used at startup.
cliParameters.opencode.thinking.label: Thinking output
cliParameters.opencode.thinking.descript: Request thinking events in structured chat output.
cliParameters.opencode.autoApprove.label: Automatic approval
cliParameters.opencode.autoApprove.descr: Automatically approve OpenCode actions; use only in trusted workspaces.
cliParameters.common.values.low.label: Low
cliParameters.common.values.medium.label: Medium
cliParameters.common.values.high.label: High
cliParameters.common.values.xhigh.label: Extra high
cliParameters.common.values.max.label: Maximum
cliParameters.common.values.max.descript: Use the maximum supported reasoning effort.
floating.title: VaneHub Assistant
floating.noSessionHint: Create or select a session in the main window first.
floating.openMainHint: Select a session in the main window
floating.emptyChat: No messages yet. Start the conversation here.
floating.returnMain: Open current session in the main window
floating.settingsDescription: Keep a desktop entry point to your sessions after the main window is closed or minimized.
floating.enableHint: When enabled, the main window close button hides the window instead of exiting VaneHub.
floating.windowsAvailable: Includes quick actions, main-window wake-up, and a mini chat using the current session configuration.
floating.windowsOnly: The desktop floating assistant is currently available in the Windows desktop app only.
